title: Drepananthus deltoideus
intro: Drepananthus deltoideus, also known as the Tawa or Bintan, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant is prized for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Drepananthus deltoideus include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit potent cytotoxic and anti-cancer activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures.
taxonomy: Drepananthus deltoideus is classified as a species within the plant family Annonaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples or soursops. Drepananthus deltoideus is native to the tropical regions of Australia. Its classification can be further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The species is also classified as a member of the genus Drepananthus.
